Historical Context: Began with Russia's annexation of Crimea and Donbas proxy war after Ukraine's pro-EU pivot; escalated to full invasion Feb 2022. Deadliest conflict of the 2020s — 440,000+ deaths.cnn
Parties: Russia vs. Ukraine; NATO arms/intel backing Kyiv; Belarus as Russian staging ground; North Korean troops/munitions for Russia.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): Highest-intensity active war on earth: 80,205 deaths in 2025, 25,842+ in 2026.cnn Ukraine retook more ground than it lost in April and May 2026 for two straight months.britannica But Russia escalated massive strikes on Kyiv, told foreigners to evacuate, and the UN warned of a "death spiral" spiralling "out of control."chathamhouse+1 Putin publicly claims the war is "approaching its conclusion."washingtoninstitute
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: Grinding stalemate with a negotiated-freeze trajectory, not a clean victory. Ukraine's two-month tactical momentum is real but localized; it does not change the strategic reality that neither side can fully expel the other. The simultaneous escalation in strikes-on-Kyiv and peace-conclusion rhetoric is the classic signature of both sides maneuvering for leverage ahead of talks. Most likely 12-month outcome: a fragile, Korea-style armistice along contested lines, leaving occupied territory de facto Russian and unresolved — a frozen conflict that reignites. Wildcard: NATO declaring readiness to defend allied territory raises real spillover/Article 5 risk if Russian strikes hit a member state.washingtoninstitute

Conflict: Middle East Crisis / Iran–Israel & Arab–Israeli wars
Historical Context: Umbrella conflict from Israel's founding, now fused into a regional war spanning Gaza, Lebanon, Syria, Yemen, and a direct Iran–Israel–US war.cnn
Parties: Israel & US vs. Iran, Hamas, Hezbollah, Houthis, allied militias; Gulf states drawn in via strikes on Bahrain, Saudi, Qatar, UAE, etc.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 28,111 deaths in 2025; 8,597 in 2026.cnn A direct Iran war erupted in 2026: US strikes inside Iran, US service members killed in Saudi Arabia, strikes on Beirut/Tehran.healthpolicy-watch Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed; his son Mojtaba Khamenei named supreme leader.healthpolicy-watch A ceasefire was ordered early April 2026; Rubio insists the war is "over," but Iran warns of "many more surprises" and accuses the US of moving to restart it.rescue+1 US House voted 215–208 (Jun 3) to curb Trump's Iran campaign.rescue
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: Officially "over," practically a hot pause that can reignite within days. The killing of Khamenei and a dynastic succession to his son is destabilizing in ways the ceasefire papers over — succession in a wounded, humiliated Islamic Republic is a powder keg, and Mojtaba lacks his father's clerical legitimacy. Iran's "surprises" rhetoric plus US "covert moves" accusations mean the ceasefire is a tactical reload, not a settlement. Highest strategic danger on this list: a Hormuz closure or renewed strikes would spike oil globally. Gaza fighting is winding down but West Bank/Lebanon flashpoints persist. Expect intermittent re-escalation through 2026; durable peace is not on the table.

Historical Context: Decades of civil wars; current war erupted April 2023 between the army and its former paramilitary partner. Now the world's worst humanitarian crisis — 24.6M in acute hunger, 2M facing famine, 11.8M+ displaced.bti-project
Parties: SAF (army, Gen. Burhan) vs. RSF (Hemedti) + a parallel RSF government in Darfur; external backers funneling weapons/mercenaries (incl. Colombian fighters) to both.aljazeerayoutubebti-project
Current Status (Jun 2026): 21,691–80,000+ deaths in 2025.cnn RSF captured El Fasher (Oct 2025) — the army's last Darfur stronghold — making RSF the dominant player.youtube Kordofan is now the epicenter; SAF retook Dilling after a 2-year siege.aljazeera All mediation has failed.aljazeera Both sides commit war crimes; RSF using starvation as a weapon.bti-project
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: De facto partition into two warlord states with no end in sight. This is the most under-covered catastrophe on the list — "an abandoned crisis."youtube The RSF's Darfur government plus its El Fasher win signals the country is fracturing along a SAF-east / RSF-west line rather than reunifying. External sponsors (Gulf actors, mercenary pipelines) keep both sides resourced, which structurally guarantees a long war. Realistic 12-month path: continued territorial consolidation by RSF in the west, brutal sieges in Kordofan, deepening famine deaths that will dwarf battle deaths, and a hardening partition. Genocide risk in Darfur is acute.

Current Status (Jun 2026): Gaza's intensive phase has wound down per Crisis Group, but West Bank spillover and anti-Hamas/anti-PA insurgencies continue.aljazeera+1
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: De-escalating tactically but politically unresolved. Post-war Gaza governance is a vacuum; absent a credible political track, low-grade insurgency and periodic flare-ups are near-certain.

Historical Context: Military junta seized power Feb 2021; ethnic armed organizations + a pro-democracy resistance launched a nationwide war.
Parties: Junta (Tatmadaw) vs. PDF resistance + ethnic armies (Arakan, Karen, Kachin); spillover into NE India.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 15,699 deaths in 2025.cnn Resistance has seized large rural/border areas; junta increasingly relies on airpower and conscription.
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: Slow-motion collapse of the junta's territorial control, but no clean rebel victory. The military is losing ground but retains airpower and the cities. Most likely path: continued fragmentation into a patchwork of EAO/resistance-controlled zones — effective de facto partition. China's interest in border stability is the main external brake.

Conflict: War in the Sahel / Maghreb Islamist Insurgencies
Historical Context: Al-Qaeda/ISIS-affiliated insurgencies (JNIM, ISGS, Boko Haram/ISWAP) across the Sahel; collapse of Western counterterror partnerships and rise of Russia-aligned juntas.
Parties: Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso juntas (+ Russian/Wagner-successor forces) vs. JNIM, ISGS, Boko Haram/ISWAP.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 21,185 deaths in 2025; 10,466+ already in 2026 — one of the fastest-rising death tolls.cnn
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: The world's most rapidly worsening jihadist theater. Burkina Faso alone is among the deadliest. The juntas' pivot from Western to Russian support has not improved security — arguably the opposite. Trajectory: continued state retreat from rural areas, jihadist encirclement of capitals (Bamako threatened), and possible state collapse in Burkina Faso/Mali. Coastal states (Benin, Togo) increasingly exposed.

Historical Context: Decades of conflict in eastern Congo over minerals, ethnicity, and Rwandan involvement.
Parties: DRC army (FARDC) + allies vs. M23 (Rwanda-backed), ADF (ISIS-aligned), dozens of militias; Rwanda–DRC interstate dimension.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 7,754–10,000+ deaths in 2025; M23 has seized major eastern cities.cnn
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: A regional war wearing a civil-war mask. The DRC–Rwanda dimension makes this more dangerous than a pure insurgency. M23's territorial gains and mineral capture entrench Rwandan leverage. Peace deals repeatedly collapse; expect continued M23 consolidation in the Kivus and persistent interstate brinkmanship.

Historical Context: Decades of state collapse; current phase is the government + AU forces vs. al-Shabaab, now with an ISIS-Puntland front.
Parties: Somali govt + ATMIS/AU + US strikes vs. al-Shabaab, ISIS-Somalia; Jubaland/Puntland constitutional crises.cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 10,783 deaths in 2025.cnn Al-Shabaab resilient; internal federal-state disputes weaken the government.
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: Permanent low-grade war. Al-Shabaab cannot take Mogadishu but cannot be eliminated; AU drawdown risks ceding ground. Trajectory: indefinite stalemate, periodic al-Shabaab offensives, fragile clan-federal politics.

Historical Context: Government's militarized campaign against cartels; included as armed conflict due to paramilitary-scale cartel forces.cnn
Parties: Mexican state vs. Sinaloa Cartel, CJNG, Gulf Cartel, Los Zetas (+ intra-cartel wars).cnn
Current Status (Jun 2026): 8,699–10,767 deaths in 2025.cnn Sinaloa's internal war (post-leadership arrests) is especially bloody; US designated cartels as terrorist orgs, raising intervention talk.
Unfiltered Forward Assessment: Endemic, with rising US-intervention risk. No "end" — it's a chronic condition. The new variable is US pressure (terrorist designations, threats of unilateral strikes), which could trigger a cross-border crisis. Cartel fragmentation tends to increase violence short-term.
